comment.body:
thank you Alma for the post, this is how I read it: Trump missed the optimal window to strike Iran, and any intervention now would likely backfire, triggering regional chaos, oil shocks, stagflation in the West, and strengthening the very regime it aims to weaken.

If Turmp still choose to strike Iran, to the market, this means: oil up, precious metal up, inflation up, credit spread up, equity down.

Is my understanding in the right direction?

comment.body:
Since the US has already allocated the navy and air forces to the region, so a strike is likely imminent. Although Trump missed the optimal time window, he'll still do it.

From military perspective, since Trump already missed the optimal time window, and Iran is more unified and prepared for war, if Trump still decides to strike Iran, I guess it'll not be a quick one (limited, contained), but a prolonged regional conflict. Which means higher inflation expectations, equity further down.

Money rotate to energy & defense, sell growth, credit spread widen --> crash come early?
